The move would borrow from the Iranian playbook that has effectively closed the Strait of...

items.title: Ситуация на фронте вместе с нашими долгосрочными санкциями и ударами средней дальности создает новую атмосферу вокруг Путина. Очень токсичная атмосфера. Только он является причиной того, что эта война началась и не закончилась. Только Путин несет ответственность за потери, которые продолжают нести россияне. Его окружение это понимает – они сигнализируют об этом нам и нашим партнерам. Российское общество тоже начинает все больше это ощущать. Нам нужны решения, которые принесут мир.
items.original_title: The situation on the front, together with our long-range sanctions and mid-range strikes, is creating a new atmosphere around Putin. A very toxic atmosphere. He alone is the reason this war began and hasn't ended. Putin alone is responsible for the losses Russians continue to suffer. His retinue understands this – they are signaling it to us and to our partners. Russian society is beginning to feel it more and more as well. We need decisions to bring about peace.
items.rss_summary:
The situation on the front, together with our long-range sanctions and mid-range strikes, is creating a new atmosphere around Putin. A very toxic atmosphere. He alone is the reason this war began and hasn't ended. Putin alone is responsible for the losses Russians continue to suffer. His retinue understands this – they are signaling it to us and to our partners. Russian society is beginning to feel it more and more as well. We need decisions to bring about peace.

items.rss_summary: By Jonathan Landay, Gram Slattery and Timour AzhariWASHINGTON/RIYADH, July 22 (Reuters) - Iranian drone attacks on CIA facilities in the Gulf have prompted U.S. intelligence analysts to investigate whether Russia assisted by providing targeting information or advanced drone technology, said four people familiar with U.S. intelligence.

items.original_title: QatarEnergy Prepares to Extend LNG Force Majeure Into October
items.rss_summary: QatarEnergy is preparing to further extend force majeure on liquefied natural gas shipments through mid-October, according to people with knowledge of the matter, in a move that would prolong the supply shock that has disrupted the global gas market amid war in the Middle East.
